lesson: Water pooling under a machine when the bowls aren't leaking is almost always a clogged condensate drain tube. Replacing bowl gaskets or shaft seals is a waste of parts.
symptoms: Water is pooling underneath the unit.
A musty, damp smell is coming from the ventilation grilles.
All bowls are cooling and turning properly.

tests.inspect-beater: Bowls and rear seals bone dry. The water pooling underneath is clear condensate, not syrup.

flavour: Cleared the pipe. No more indoor water features.
authenticityNote: Humid air condensing on the cold evaporator barrels drips into a tray and drains through a tube. If dust, sugar, or slime clogs this tube, the condensate tray overflows and drips directly under the base of the machine.
